attributes.attribute: The ordered rank of the entry.
A kebab-case string. Valid identifiers are descriptive, but concise.

Whether the entry can be safely removed upon completion, or if it is necessary context for the codebase. Dynamic in nature; any item with expendable="false" MUST be reviewed for necessity on each read of this .
The current status of the chore. Entries may only persist with a "closed" status if the expendable attribute is set to "false".

schema.examples:
These examples demonstrate the attribute and format conventions entries added to references. All entries in any MUST have values for each . Additional attributes are forbidden without an explicit in the file.
Fill this space with additional context and instructions using XML hierarchies.
Describe the scope of the plan in this space. At a minimum, describe the primary objectives that must be satisfied to consider the plan completed.
